title: Add a stint phase-change sidecar that detects tyre-cliff onset, not just absolute tyre age
summary: Predictive-maintenance literature repeatedly treats degradation as multi-phase with a change point, and F1 strategy articles explain that undercut value depends on when old tyres stop behaving linearly.
rationale: Predictive-maintenance literature repeatedly treats degradation as multi-phase with a change point, and F1 strategy articles explain that undercut value depends on when old tyres stop behaving linearly. Current features mostly encode level and simple ratios; they do not explicitly estimate whether a stint just crossed from stable to cliff-like behavior.
implementation_hint: Within each `Year-Race-Driver-Stint`, compute rolling slopes/curvature of `LapTime_Delta`, `LapTime (s)`, and `Cumulative_Degradation` against `TyreLife` or lap index. Derive `phase_change_score`, `recent_acceleration_in_loss`, `pre_cliff_vs_post_cliff` bins, and optionally a tiny sidecar model that predicts change-point probability; feed its OOF output into the main learner.
expected_effect: Better separation between old-but-still-acceptable tyres and old tyres that have just entered the real pit window.
risk: Per-stint sequences are short; noisy lap deltas or safety-car laps can create false change points unless status-filtered first.
